event_blocks.summary: The video begins with an introduction from Tracy for her 'Country Charm by Tracy' channel, promoting Easter and Spring DIY crafts, followed by a call-to-action screen. Transitioning to the 'Easter & Spring DIY Crafts Collection #4,' the scene shifts to a crafting workspace featuring a grid cutting mat, a large wooden heart, white beads, twine, checkered burlap ribbon, artificial flowers, and pink paint. Tracy explains that hearts can be used year-round, especially for springtime, and that her wooden hearts are from the Crafter's Square section of the Dollar Tree, suggesting alternatives from Valentine's Day sections. The crafter then applies two coats of white chalk paint to the large wooden heart, followed by a 'burlap' colored acrylic paint around the edges using a stencil brush for a rustic farmhouse look, utilizing a white paint tray and a bottle of gold paint. She then outlines the heart's edges with a fine Sharpie marker for a doodled effect and splatters black paint onto the edges using a stiff stencil brush. Next, she hot glues black-and-white checkered burlap ribbon from Hobby Lobby to the bottom edge of the heart to create a pocket. The crafter uses an orange-and-black hot glue gun and red-handled scissors to trim excess ribbon, and a pink-handled tool to smooth the edges. She explains how to create holes in the soft wood heart using the pink-handled 'poking tool' for wire. She then threads thin, silver wire from the Dollar Tree's automotive section through a hole in the heart and uses orange-handled pliers to cut the wire, onto which she strings ten white beads, five on each side, creating decorative strands. On-screen text directs viewers to the description box for tool information. The crafter then trims stems from artificial flowers, including 'cherry blossoms,' 'pink wildflowers,' 'boxwood' greenery, and 'white berries' resembling baby's breath, sourced from Dollar Tree and Walmart. She applies hot glue to the back of the heart and attaches the foliage and flowers into the burlap pocket on one side, planning to adhere another heart on the other side. Small white beads, identified as 'pit berry little sections' from Hobby Lobby, are threaded onto wire and placed along the top curve of the heart.

Crafting Workspace.description: A top-down view of a grid cutting mat serving as the crafting surface, with tools and materials neatly arranged around the heart.

Pink Paint Bottle.description: Not visible in this segment; replaced by a gold paint bottle labeled 'DecoArt Americana' used for outlining the heart.

Artificial Flowers.description: A collection of artificial green foliage, white baby’s breath, and pink blossoms placed on the crafting mat, identified by the crafter as 'cherry blossoms from the Dollar Tree,' 'pink wildflowers,' 'boxwood' greenery from Walmart, and 'white berries' that 'look like baby’s breath' also from Walmart, which she uses to 'fill up my pockets' on the heart.

Easter & Spring DIY Crafts Collection #4.description: A pink and white checkered background with animated bunnies, flowers, and text introducing the craft collection, featuring playful Easter-themed graphics, which the host Tracy describes as part of her 'Easter and Spring DIYs' and 'floral ideas'.

Large Wooden Heart.description: A white-painted wooden heart with a distressed finish, now being decorated with a checkered burlap ribbon along its bottom edge, and being prepared for wire and bead embellishment, with the crafter explaining she will 'adhere my heart' with the floral arrangement.

raw_segments: The video opens with an upbeat, gentle acoustic background music as a title card for 'Country Charm by Tracy' appears, featuring a rustic design with green foliage and text promoting handpainting, doodles, wreaths, and home decor. A female voice, Tracy, introduces herself, saying, "Hey friends, it's Tracy. Welcome to another collection video of my Easter and Spring DIYs." This transitions to a call-to-action screen with a 'SUBSCRIBE' button, social media icons, and a link tree prompt, all framed by the same leafy border. Tracy continues, "Today I'm sharing some floral ideas that I have here on my channel, and I'm so glad that you're here. Without further ado, let's get started." The scene then shifts to a vibrant pink and white checkered background with animated bunnies and flowers, introducing the 'Easter & Spring DIY Crafts Collection #4.' The background music continues as the next segment displays a top-down view of a crafting workspace on a grid cutting mat, featuring various materials including a large wooden heart, a box of white beads, twine, black-and-white checkered burlap ribbon, heart-shaped ornaments, pink and white artificial flowers, and a small bottle of pink paint. Tracy begins discussing her supplies, stating, "So hearts are not just for Valentines. You can definitely use them anytime throughout the year, especially for the springtime. Now, what I'm starting with here are my supplies that I got from, you know, different craft stores." The camera then zooms in on the large wooden heart as Tracy specifies, "Now, what I'm starting with is one of these wood hearts, and these are in the Crafter's Square section of the Dollar Tree." She adds, "If you do not or are not fortunate enough to find these, you can use maybe some leftover hearts that you have from the Valentine section," as the scene shows a side-by-side comparison of the large heart and a stack of smaller wooden hearts with red ribbons, suggesting a crafting project in progress.

The segment opens with a top-down view of a crafting workspace, featuring a grid cutting mat, a large wooden heart, and a stack of smaller wooden hearts with red ribbons. A female voice explains the project requires some form, introducing it as a "spring DIY" that can also be used for "Valentine leftover crafts." The scene then transitions to a close-up as hands begin to paint the large wooden heart with white paint, applying it evenly across the surface. The speaker confirms she is applying "two coats of white chalk paint" to the heart, going around the edges and then painting the front. After the white base coat is applied, the hands switch to a different paint, carefully outlining the edges of the heart. The speaker identifies this as an "acrylic paint" in "the color burlap," used to achieve a "rustic farmhouse look," describing how she uses a "stencil brush" to dab, stipple, and blend the paint around the heart. The workspace includes a white palette with paint wells, a bottle of gold paint labeled 'DecoArt Americana' (which the speaker refers to as the burlap-colored acrylic), and a blue cloth for cleaning the brush. The bright, consistent lighting emphasizes the crafting process as the heart transforms from plain wood to a painted, embellished piece.

The video opens with a top-down view of a crafting workspace, where a large wooden heart is being painted white by the crafter, who explains she's applying paint 'just in different places, kind of like where it would be kind of like a board, uh or shiplap kind of look.' She then switches to a fine Sharpie marker to carefully outline the heart’s edges, stating she's doing this 'just to give it a bit of doodling because I like that look.' Following this, she uses a stiff stencil brush to apply black paint to the edges, describing her action as splattering the heart with black paint, noting, 'You can totally cut out any of these steps that you do not like.' The scene transitions to a black-and-white checkered burlap ribbon, labeled from Hobby Lobby, laid out on the grid cutting mat. The crafter applies hot glue to the back of the burlap ribbon, explaining, 'I don't like the edges uh, you know, those seams, so I'm just hot gluing those down,' before carefully attaching it to the bottom edge of the painted wooden heart to create 'a little pocket for my card.' The organized workspace, with a white paint tray and gold paint bottle visible, and the crafter’s hands with pink-painted nails, are seen throughout, as upbeat instrumental background music begins to play.

The minute opens with a top-down view of a crafting workspace, where a person with bright pink-painted nails is meticulously attaching a black-and-white checkered burlap ribbon to the bottom edge of a large, distressed white wooden heart, all set against a backdrop of gentle, acoustic instrumental music. The crafter, working on a grid cutting mat, first positions the ribbon, then uses an orange and black hot glue gun to apply adhesive to its back before pressing it firmly into place. Red-handled scissors are then used to neatly trim the excess fabric. As the crafter employs a pink-handled tool, likely a seam ripper or awl, to carefully tuck and smooth the ribbon's edges along the heart’s curved contour, a female voice begins to speak. She shares a useful tip, explaining her intention to put two holes in the heart for a wire. She clarifies that while an electric drill could be used, she's sharing a specific technique because these hearts are made of very soft wood. She then refers to the pink-handled tool as her 'poking tool,' noting that with 'a little elbow grease, a little pressure,' it works nicely for this task. The organized workspace, with the hot glue gun, scissors, and a small container of glue visible, underscores the precision and attention to detail throughout the process.

The video continues with a top-down view of the Crafting Workspace, where the crafter, whose hands with pink-painted nails are visible, is meticulously working on the Large Wooden Heart. The heart, previously painted white with a distressed finish, is being prepared for further embellishment. The crafter uses a pink-handled tool, which she refers to as a 'poking tool,' to carefully tuck and smooth the checkered burlap ribbon along the curved bottom edge of the heart, ensuring a neat and secure fit. She explains that this tool pokes through the wood quite nicely, offering it as an alternative for creating holes if a drill isn't available, and mentions a link in the description for this tool, noting viewers can find it in a craft store or on Amazon. After adjusting the ribbon, the crafter sets the tool aside and picks up a thin, metallic wire, which she identifies as 'silver wire' from the Dollar Tree, specifically from the automotive section. She threads this wire through a small hole near the top of the heart. A small wooden box containing several white beads is then introduced to the workspace. The crafter uses a pair of orange-handled pliers to cut the wire to length and proceeds to thread the white beads onto the wire, creating a decorative strand. She further details that this wire used to come in silver, red, and black, but now primarily red and black. Throughout this process, on-screen text appears, reading 'Check description box BELOW for info on Tools I use,' reinforcing the crafter's verbal guidance. The overall atmosphere remains focused and methodical, emphasizing the detailed handcrafting process.

The segment opens with a top-down view of the crafting workspace, where the crafter’s hands, adorned with bright pink nail polish, are actively threading small, round white beads onto a thin metallic wire. A female voice, identified as the crafter, describes the wire she uses as 'soft, pliable, and bendable,' noting she particularly likes its 'grade.' She explains her process, stating she's using '10 wood beads,' intending to place 'five on one side and five on the other,' and demonstrates bending the wire to position them. As soft, melodic instrumental music begins to play, the crafter uses Orange-Handled Pliers to cut the wire to the desired length, creating two separate beaded strands. These strands are then carefully positioned on either side of the Large Wooden Heart, which is already partially decorated with a checkered burlap ribbon along its bottom edge. The crafter meticulously tucks and smooths the ribbon along the heart’s edge using a pink-handled tool. A Small Wooden Box containing additional white beads is visible nearby. As the segment progresses, the crafter introduces a variety of artificial flowers—green foliage, white baby’s breath, and pink blossoms—placing them on the organized grid cutting mat, suggesting the next phase of decoration. On-screen text appears in the upper left corner, reading 'Check description box BELOW for info on Tools I use,' providing viewers with additional resource information.

The crafter, with pink-painted nails, continues the crafting process on the Large Wooden Heart, now focusing on floral decoration. She uses purple-handled wire cutters to trim stems from a bundle of Artificial Flowers, which she identifies as "cherry blossoms from the Dollar Tree," "pink wildflowers," and "greenery from Walmart" (specifically "boxwood"), along with "white berries" that "look like baby’s breath" also sourced from Walmart. She states that "now the fun part begins for me" as she takes these flowers to "start filling up my pockets" on the heart. After trimming, the crafter applies hot glue from the Hot Glue Gun to the back of the heart, meticulously attaching the green foliage and flowers in a natural, layered fashion, explaining she "just glue everything into the pocket until I like the look." She clarifies her arrangement strategy, noting she's putting the flowers "to one side because on the other side that's where I'm going to adhere my heart." Small, round White Beads, which she identifies as "pit berry little sections" from Hobby Lobby that she purchased as a garland and took apart, are threaded onto a wire and carefully positioned along the top curve of the heart, adding a delicate, decorative touch. The checkered burlap ribbon remains secured along the bottom edge, and the crafter uses a pink-handled tool to tuck and smooth the ribbon for a clean finish. The crafting workspace remains organized, with a stack of smaller wooden hearts visible to the right, and on-screen text in the upper left corner advises viewers to "Check description box BELOW for info on Tools I use." The overall atmosphere is calm and methodical, emphasizing precision and aesthetic appeal in the DIY project.

event_blocks.summary: In a crafting workspace, a crafter with pink-painted nails meticulously decorates a large wooden heart, already white and distressed. Using a hot glue gun, she attaches artificial flowers—pink blossoms, green foliage, and white baby’s breath—along with a black-and-white checkered burlap ribbon. White beads are threaded onto wire to form a decorative loop, which is then cut with orange-handled pliers. Upbeat instrumental music plays as a pink-handled tool smooths the burlap ribbon along the heart's curve. The finished heart is then lifted. The scene shifts to a 'LOLC DIY ORNAMENT' pack, identified as a 'color your own ornament pack from Dollar Tree' from the Valentine season. The crafter sands a bright pink foam heart with a black sanding block, encouraging viewers to 'think outside the box' and use hearts beyond Valentine's Day. She further explains painting the wooden heart with 'Royal Fuchsia Americana paint' and sanding the edges. A bright pink foam heart is attached to the upper right of the wooden heart for added height, secured with red cardstock glued to its back using a hot glue gun. Finally, twine, described as 'jewelry cording or hemp cording,' is unrolled and knotted. Artificial flowers, white beads, and wire remain visible in the crafting workspace, and on-screen text reminds viewers to check the description for tool information.

event_blocks.summary: Hands with pink-painted nails tie a six-loop twine bow on a grid crafting mat, with a female voice explaining the process and securing it with a knot, then attaching the bow to a white-painted wooden heart decorated with white beads on wire, a pink foam heart, checkered burlap ribbon, and artificial pink blossoms and white baby’s breath. Upbeat instrumental music plays as an 'Easter Bucket Floral DIY' title card appears. A new crafting setup on a whitewashed wooden surface displays a bag of preserved Spanish moss, a galvanized metal bucket, artificial pink and green flowers, a white bunny figurine on a stick, and wooden beads. A hand picks up a can of Loctite Polyurethane Foam, and the female voice identifies the bunny figurine as from Dollar Tree, an Easter pick from Hobby Lobby, and the Spanish moss from Walmart, stating her intention to fill the bucket. The crafter sprays Loctite Polyurethane Foam into a small galvanized metal bucket, which expands and fills it before being cut and shaped. The speaker explains her preference for this foam over Styrofoam for filling containers, referencing a previous issue with a different can. White tacky glue is applied to the foam with a wooden stick, and green preserved Spanish moss is pressed in to create a grassy base. Artificial greenery, pastel eggs, wooden beads, and a white bunny figurine are arranged and inserted into the moss, with stems trimmed using black-handled scissors and red-handled wire cutters. The workspace is cluttered with tools like wire cutters, scissors, and an Aleene's Tacky Glue bottle. The final product, a charming Easter bucket centerpiece, is displayed on a white rotating turntable.

A female voice refers to it as "this tight foam" and discusses a previous experience with "a bad can" where "the gaps and cracks did not come out... the way that it should," explaining she uses "this" foam as an alternative to Styrofoam for filling containers, and that "a lot of floral designers" use "the gaps and crack" foam, letting it dry for several hours or overnight.

event_blocks.summary: The crafter’s hands, with bright pink nail polish, remove a tag from a bundle of artificial white gypsophila flowers, referred to as “Gypso,” explaining they are from the Dollar Tree and are essential for the arrangement. Working in a crafting space featuring a brown paper table cover and a whitewashed wooden backdrop, the crafter, dressed in a pink shirt and white apron, uses purple-handled wire cutters to trim the gypsophila stems. These flowers are then added to a galvanized metal bucket, identified as a medium tin bucket from Hobby Lobby, which already contains green excelsior, artificial greenery, pink cherry blossoms, and speckled Easter eggs, with a white bunny cutout positioned centrally. Amidst upbeat, folksy instrumental music, the crafter meticulously arranges the white gypsophila around the bunny, appreciating their “wispiness” for adding an “extra touch” to the complete arrangement, which includes the cherry blossoms, greenery, and bunny. She also expresses contentment with selecting the medium tin bucket over a smaller alternative. After rotating the bucket for a full inspection, a social media overlay with Instagram and Facebook handles (@countrycharmbytracy and @countrycharmcommunity) briefly appears. The scene shifts outdoors to display the finished bucket arrangement against a beige brick wall and green shrubs, followed by a concluding title card, 'Spring Tin Bucket Floral DIY,' set against a pink and white checkered background with animated bunnies and flowers.

Artificial Flowers (New Set).description: A bundle of artificial white gypsophila flowers with green stems, referred to by the crafter as 'Gypso' and noted as being from the Dollar Tree, being trimmed and inserted into the bucket to enhance the arrangement’s fullness, with the crafter expressing her love for their 'wispiness'.

Excelsior (Green Color).description: A layer of green-dyed wood fiber at the base of the bucket, simulating grass and providing a natural foundation for the arrangement, referred to by the crafter as part of 'the greenery'.

event_blocks.summary: On a whitewashed wooden surface, a crafter with bright pink nail polish begins a spring arrangement, introducing pink flowers from Walmart and a galvanized metal bucket from the Dollar Tree labeled 'FLOWERS & GARDEN'. She explains the use of 'styrofoam' and 'pit berries' from Hobby Lobby, which she detaches from a garland for rustic touches. Her hands then open a clear plastic bag of natural-colored excelsior, described as 'fine excelsior' resembling hay or Spanish moss, and fluff it into the bucket as a textured base. Artificial pink roses and white berry branches are laid out beside the bucket, ready for arrangement, while the on-screen text 'CountryCharmbyTracy' is visible in the bottom left corner under bright lighting.

event_blocks.summary: On a whitewashed wooden surface, a crafter's hands, distinguished by bright pink nail polish, meticulously assemble an artificial floral arrangement. The crafter, speaking in a female voice, begins by inserting artificial pink roses with green stems into a galvanized metal bucket containing green floral foam and light beige excelsior, noting her enjoyment of "layering them, stacking them up." She then incorporates white berry branches, referred to as "white pit berries," trimming their brown stems and securing them into the foam with a hot glue gun, explaining that these berries contribute "a lot of softness" to her "country rustic projects." She further details her method, mentioning the use of a "still picks machine" to secure "florals in place in the styrofoam." Additional pink roses are added, building a full, cascading effect, and the bucket is rotated for even distribution. The completed arrangement, a lush, spring-themed display of pink roses, white berries, and green foliage, is presented on a lace doily, described by the crafter as the "perfect little floral arrangement to add a bit of pink and spring into my craft room." During the final moments, on-screen text 'CountryCharmbyTracy' appears in the bottom left, and a 'Subscribe for More Crafty Ideas' call-to-action is displayed in the top right, all underscored by gentle, upbeat acoustic background music.

event_blocks.summary: The crafting process begins with a wooden bunny cutout placed on a whitewashed wooden surface, where the crafter applies Mod Podge using a paintbrush, paying special attention to the edges. The bunny is then placed on colorful striped paper, and its outline is traced with a purple pen. The crafter uses a mini heat press, identified as a compact white iron with a red base, to adhere burlap fabric to a paper bunny cutout on a black Cricut cutting mat, ensuring the bunny is secured and edges are covered. After ironing, the burlap reveals the imprinted bunny shape, which is then traced with a black marker for cutting. Three identical burlap bunny shapes are cut using black-handled scissors. Wooden letters spelling 'HOP' are traced onto white cardstock to create a template, then painted purple ('lavender') with a paintbrush from a 'DecoArt Americana' bottle. Three small burlap flags are painted with pink ('Poodle Skirt Pink') from another 'DecoArt Americana' bottle. The painted wooden letters are glued onto the burlap flags using a hot glue gun. Mini wooden baskets from Hobby Lobby are used, and the burlap flags are placed inside one. Natural twine is tied around the basket's handle, forming a decorative bow. Green floral foam is glued into the basket's base, and light beige excelsior is added to cover it. Artificial greenery with small purple flowers from Hobby Lobby is trimmed with red-handled wire cutters and arranged in the basket. A colorful striped paper bunny cutout is attached to the front with twine, and the burlap flags spelling 'HOP' with purple wooden letters adorn the basket, creating a spring-themed decorative piece.

The scene transitions to a title card with a pink and white checkered background, animated bunnies, and sparkling effects, displaying the text 'BUNNY HOP Decor' in blue cursive. A female voice, the crafter, introduces the project, stating, "Okay, for this cute project is a Bunny Hop decor piece that I created." The crafting process begins with a wooden bunny cutout placed on the same whitewashed wooden surface, which the crafter identifies as "one of these wood pieces from the Dollar Tree." She explains her method, "I'm also going to be using the Mod Podge heat transfer method," as her hands, with bright pink nail polish, apply Mod Podge with a paintbrush to the bunny. She details this step, explaining, "first I put a coat of Mod Podge on the wood bunny. The crafter then places the bunny on a colorful striped paper, and using a purple pen, she begins to trace its outline, explaining her intention: "So, I want to capture um a specific part of the paper, so I just take my pen, something that I can see and then just um carefully draw around the bunny and then I will cut it out with scissors." The environment maintains a consistent rustic, country-chic aesthetic, with the on-screen text 'CountryCharmbyTracy' visible in the bottom left corner, reinforcing the brand identity. The background music continues throughout.

event_blocks.summary: The crafter’s hands, with bright pink nail polish, meticulously arrange artificial flowers on a whitewashed wooden surface, securing a mini wooden basket with burlap flags spelling 'HOP' to a bunny using E6000 and hot glue for a permanent hold. She adjusts purple blooms, green foliage, and white baby's breath for a full, cascading arrangement, trimming sprigs with red-handled scissors. A decorative bow is crafted by layering purple polka-dotted ribbon, natural burlap, and silver glitter ribbon, secured and trimmed with red-handled scissors, then tied with white lace ribbon. raw_segments: The video opens with a top-down view of a whitewashed wooden crafting surface, where the crafter’s hands, adorned with bright pink nail polish, are meticulously arranging artificial flowers. The speaker's calm female voice explains her process, noting she's securing the small, light-colored wooden basket—which features burlap flags painted pink with purple wooden letters spelling 'HOP'—to the front of a bunny, ensuring it stands up proportionally. She details using a combination of E6000 and hot glue for a permanent hold. The crafter carefully adjusts the purple blooms and green foliage, specifically mentioning the 'white baby's breath' she sourced from Walmart, to create a full, cascading, and balanced arrangement for the bunny. Using red-handled scissors, she trims sprigs of flowers and later strips of burlap. The focus then shifts to crafting a decorative bow, intended for the bunny's ear. The crafter layers a piece of purple polka-dotted ribbon, identified by the speaker as a 'purple small dot polka-dot ribbon' from Hobby Lobby, over natural burlap, then adds a shimmering silver glitter ribbon, which the speaker confirms is 'glittery.' These layers are secured, likely with hot glue, and neatly trimmed with red-handled scissors to form a symmetrical bow. Finally, a delicate white lace ribbon, also from Hobby Lobby, is used to tie the bow, adding an elegant finish. Throughout the precise, deliberate DIY process, the on-screen text 'CountryCharmbyTracy' remains visible in the bottom left corner.

The video segment opens with a close-up of the crafter’s hands, adorned with bright pink nail polish, meticulously assembling a decorative bow using purple polka-dotted, silver glitter, white lace, and burlap ribbons. The crafter explains, "I just criss-cross those until I like the look," as she shapes and adjusts the bow on a whitewashed wooden surface, with red-handled pliers visible nearby. She adds that she gets "the best hold with a uh pipe cleaner," which she puts around the bow, not worrying about it being seen because she's "going to... glue a cute flower to the center of this messy bow." The bow is then attached to a colorful striped paper bunny cutout, which serves as the centerpiece of a small floral arrangement in a mini wooden basket. The basket is filled with artificial greenery and small purple and white flowers, and features burlap flags with 'HOP' spelled out in purple wooden letters. The crafter then adds vibrant, multi-colored foam flowers from a package labeled 'EASTER' to the top of the bunny’s head, securing them with a hot glue gun. She reveals that "These flowers came from nowhere else but Hobby Lobby in the Easter uh section," and that she "just glued it there in the center of that bow," believing it to be "the perfect addition to this bunny hop decor piece that I created." The completed craft, with "CountryCharmbyTracy" text on-screen, is then displayed in a rustic home setting, surrounded by other Easter decorations including burlap bags, painted eggs, and greenery, creating a charming springtime display. Cheerful, upbeat instrumental music begins playing as the segment concludes with a cheerful animated end screen featuring pink and white checkered borders, cartoon bunnies, flowers, and the text 'Thanks for watching...' in blue cursive, signaling the end of the tutorial.

The segment concludes with a cheerful, static end screen, perfectly designed to wrap up a crafting tutorial. The background features a soft pink color with a pink-and-white checkered border at the top and bottom, evoking a delightful springtime or Easter theme. Animated cartoon bunnies peek into the frame from the corners: one on the left holds a pink polka-dotted egg and a blue egg, while the one on the right holds a yellow and purple striped egg. Both bunnies are white with pink inner ears and rosy cheeks, adding to the playful aesthetic. Small clusters of pink and white flowers with green leaves and tiny hearts further decorate the top corners. In the center, the text 'Thanks for watching...' is displayed in a friendly, blue cursive font with a subtle shadow, surrounded by gentle white sparkles that give a magical, celebratory feel. Throughout this visual, light, cheerful, and upbeat instrumental background music plays, enhancing the warm, inviting, and joyful farewell atmosphere aligned with the Easter and spring crafting theme.
